arguments.name: Meeting Prep Summary (every 30 min)
arguments.prompt:
You are running an automated meeting-prep routine. Follow every step below completely before sending the email.

## Step 1 — Find the next upcoming meeting
Use google-calendar.list_calendars to get all calendars, then google-calendar.list_events on the primary calendar with time_min = now (UTC ISO-8601) and time_max = 24 hours from now, max_results = 10. Pick the single next upcoming event (earliest start time in the future). Extract:
- Meeting title
- Start/end time
- Attendees and their organizations (infer company from email domains; exclude your own domain)
- Meeting description / notes

If no upcoming meetings are found in the next 24 hours, do nothing — do not send an email.

## Step 2 — Identify the company
From the attendees' email domains and the meeting title/description, identify the primary external company name. If multiple external companies are present, pick the most prominent one.

## Step 3 — Search Google Drive for relevant files
Use google-drive.list_files with search queries targeting the company name and meeting context. Try at least two queries:
1. fullText contains '"COMPANY_NAME"'
2. name contains 'COMPANY_NAME'
Download (google-drive.download_file) the top 3 most relevant files and extract key facts: deal status, open issues, last contact, pricing, contracts, notes, etc.

## Step 4 — Fetch latest web news
Use builtin.http to fetch recent news about the company. Try:
- https://news.google.com/rss/search?q=COMPANY_NAME&hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US:en
Parse the RSS XML for the top 5 headlines (title + source + pubDate + link).

## Step 5 — Compose and send the meeting-prep email
Send (via your configured Email delivery target) a well-structured HTML-friendly email with these sections:

**Subject:** Meeting Prep: [Meeting Title] with [Company] — [Date & Time UTC]

**Body sections:**
1. 📅 **Meeting Overview** — title, time, attendees
2. 🏢 **Company Snapshot** — what you know about this company from Drive files (deal stage, key contacts, context, open items)
3. 📰 **Latest News** — top 5 headlines with links, source, and date
4. ✅ **Suggested Talking Points** — 3–5 bullet points synthesized from the Drive context and news
5. 📁 **Source Files** — list the Drive files referenced with their names

Be concise, professional, and actionable. Use clear section headers.

response.content:
Everything is set up and running. Here's a summary of what was configured:

---

## ✅ Meeting Prep Trigger Created

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| **Trigger name** | Meeting Prep Summary (every 30 min) |
| **Schedule** | Every 30 minutes, UTC |
| **First run** | 2026-06-19 18:30 UTC |
| **Delivery target** | Email (QA trace inbox) |
| **Trigger ID** | `01KVGHW3BB6CC4WFDAZXWV6AK5` |

---

### What happens every 30 minutes:

1. **📅 Checks your Google Calendar** for the next upcoming meeting (within 24 hours)
2. **🏢 Identifies the external company** from attendee email domains and meeting details
3. **📁 Searches Google Drive** for relevant files (by company name in file names and content), downloads the top 3, and extracts key context
4. **📰 Fetches live web news** about the company via Google News RSS (top 5 headlines)
5. **📧 Sends a structured email** with:
   - Meeting overview (time, attendees)
   - Company snapshot from your Drive files
   - Latest news headlines with links
   - Suggested talking points
   - Source file references

If no upcoming meeting is found, nothing is sent. To remove or pause this trigger, just ask.
